title: from_trusted() doc invites misuse on server-origin data
description: Id::from_trusted() and UTCDate::from_trusted() docs say 'server-assigned IDs' are a valid use case. A contributor who receives any server-originated string will interpret this as license to skip new() — including relayed user content or synthetic test ids. The name 'from_trusted' does not restrict to 'deserialization only'. Maintenance consequence: every future PR that touches Id construction must be reviewed for misuse; issues about empty-string Ids reaching the wire will be hard to trace because from_trusted accepts '' silently, violating the 'Guaranteed non-empty' invariant with no debug_assert to catch it at test time.
